summary: Drew DeVault gives a brief April 2021 status update, noting that nearly all his effort has gone into his then-secret programming language project (later revealed as Hare). He directs readers to the sourcehut 'what's cooking' post for SourceHut news. He reports that a recent call for contributors brought several new people on board and narrows the remaining needs to cryptography, date/time, and networking expertise. He shares a teaser of the unix::passwd stdlib module as an example of contributor work.
key_points: The secret systems programming language project (Hare) consumed nearly all of DeVault's time this month
New contributors joined after a public call, and the project now needs help specifically with cryptography, date/time, and DNS/networking
The project explicitly requires experienced systems programmers — no bandwidth for mentoring newcomers
A stdlib API teaser (unix::passwd group entry parsing) shows the language's C-like struct syntax and explicit resource management patterns
money_quotes: I have focused almost entirely on the programming language project this month, for which the details are kept private.
We don't have the bandwidth to mentor inexperienced programmers right now, so please only reach out if you have an established background in systems programming.
These updates might be light on details for a while as we work on this project.
key_insight: DeVault's secret systems language project is rapidly building momentum with new contributors tackling core stdlib modules, though the tight-lipped development style means public updates remain deliberately sparse.
